question.wording: What does the acronym XML stand for?
Which of the following statements about XML tags is correct?

choices.choice: Closing tags can be omitted in empty elements

question.wording: How must attribute values appear in XML?

choices.choice: They can be without quotes if they are numbers

question.wording: How many root elements can a well-formed XML document have?

choices.choice: Zero, if it only contains comments

question.wording: What is the primary function of a DTD in relation to XML?

choices.choice: To give visual style to the data
To define the structure and legal rules of a document
To convert XML to an SQL database
To add security and encryption to the file

question.wording: How do you write a comment in XML?
What does it mean for an XML document to be "well-formed"?

choices.choice: It has been validated against a DTD or Schema
It follows the basic syntax rules of XML
It displays correctly in a browser

question.wording: What are Namespaces used for in XML?

choices.choice: To avoid name conflicts between elements
To speed up the file download
To limit the number of allowed tags

question.wording: What is the correct way to declare the XML version at the start of a document?
What is the main difference between XML and HTML?

choices.choice: XML transports data and HTML displays it
XML can only be used on Windows
HTML does not allow custom tags
